name: Celia Cruz
abstract: Celia Caridad Cruz Alfonso (21 October 1925 – 16 July 2003), known as Celia Cruz, was a Cuban-American singer and one of the most popular Latin artists of the 20th century. Cruz rose to fame in Cuba during the 1950s as a singer of guarachas, earning the nickname La Guarachera de Cuba.

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: Later, the second time was when I accompanied her for the first time in the Liborio in 1963. ... I accompanied her in the Chateau Madrid twice, in 1970 and 1971.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Valentin
about_quotes.evidence: Celia Cruz and a lot of guitar... So it was a combination of what we call hebajro music... and also the era of the Palladium, which my parents used to go to the Palladium
